description: Registry of experiments, architectural decisions, and key findings. Each entry records what was done, what was learned, and how it shaped the system.

experiments.name: 10-word river completion (initial session)
experiments.description: First acquisition loop: identified 10 missing Māori words from River World stories and acquired them (kua, tana, tahi, rangi, teitei, mau, whakakiia, piki, toro, atu)
experiments.actions: Acquired 10 words with entity records, surface forms, audio refs, and MP3 downloads
experiments.findings: No prediction ledger existed yet; gaps were identified manually via story-gap-analysis.js

experiments.name: 7-word final acquisition to 100%
experiments.description: Final acquisition batch: acquired remaining 7 words (pakiaka, kainga, to, mihi, nga, taku, whakautu) to reach full 49/49 coverage
experiments.initial_state: 45/49 sentences resolvable; 2 co-dependency clusters blocking 4 words
experiments.actions: Built acquisition-planner.js with 3-planner architecture. Acquired pakiaka (ENTITY_INTEGRATION, gain=1) then 6 co-dependent words (3 clusters resolved). Linked entities to experiences.js.
findings: Co-dependency clusters require all members to be acquired before any gain is realized
JOINT_ACQUISITION prediction type accurately models cluster resolution (gain=0 per individual step)
Horizon planner correctly identifies cluster resolution strategy
Planner infrastructure didn't exist for EXP-001; those 10 predictions reconstructed retroactively

experiments.name: Three-planner architecture
experiments.description: The acquisition planner uses three planners with distinct strategies, not a single optimizer
experiments.rationale: Structural planner handles immediate gain maximization. Horizon planner handles multi-step trajectory planning for co-dependency clusters. Probabilistic planner handles uncertainty via calibrated distributions.
experiments.alternatives: Single optimizer with lookahead was considered but rejected because it conflates different decision types

experiments.name: Prediction ledger as learning signal
experiments.description: All acquisition predictions are logged to .agent-prediction-ledger.json and used for confidence calibration
experiments.rationale: Without ground-truth outcomes, the planner cannot improve. The ledger provides empirical error distributions per gap type.

experiments.name: State hash for termination detection
experiments.description: A state hash is computed from current coverage state. If unchanged from last run, planner skips analysis.
experiments.rationale: Prevents redundant planner invocations when state hasn't changed (e.g., human hasn't integrated the recommendation yet).

experiments.name: Three-gap-type model
experiments.description: Gaps are classified as ENTITY_INTEGRATION, AUDIO_ACQUISITION, or JOINT_ACQUISITION (co-dependency)
experiments.rationale: Different gap types require different actions and have different prediction characteristics. ENTITY_INTEGRATION needs new entity+SF+audio+contribution. AUDIO_ACQUISITION needs only a new audio_ref. JOINT_ACQUISITION needs multiple coordinated acquisitions.

experiments.name: Language Policy: 4 voice types
experiments.description: Every surface form in the curriculum must eventually have explicit audio recordings for all four voice profiles
experiments.rationale: Learners need to hear pronunciations from different speaker types to develop robust phonetic perception. Also important for representation in language preservation.
experiments.implementation: RequirementMatrix tracks explicit/legacy/missing per voice type. AudioCoverage generates recording queues. Legacy recordings (voice_type: null) are classified via inventory tool.

experiments.name: Dual deployment structure
experiments.description: Source files live in apps/river-world/ and packages/language-data/; deployed copies live in docs/ and docs/packages/language-data/
experiments.rationale: GitHub Pages serves from docs/ as web root. Source files are the canonical location; docs/ is the deployment artefact.
experiments.complexity: Script paths differ between source (apps/river-world/ → ../../packages/) and deployment (docs/ → packages/)
experiments.known_issue: docs/index.html had wrong paths (../../packages/ instead of packages/) from EXP-001 era; fixed in commit cf66a3b

experiments.name: EXP-001 predictions cannot be reconstructed
experiments.description: The planner and prediction ledger infrastructure didn't exist during EXP-001, making those 10 predictions non-reconstructable
experiments.impact: Ledger has 17 entries (7 from EXP-002, 10 labeled with note). Prediction accuracy statistics are based on available data only.

experiments.name: Audio resolution chain
experiments.description: The complete fallback chain is: direct phrase -> entity-level -> composed -> atomic sequence -> dictionary lookup -> TTS
experiments.discovered_during: Work on story-audio-resolver.js and audio-resolution.js revealed that multiple fallback tiers are needed because different sentences resolve at different tiers

experiments.name: afrikaans-phrases.js path broken on GitHub Pages
experiments.description: docs/index.html loaded afrikaans-phrases.js via ../../packages/language-data/ which resolves outside the /maori-sre/ path on GitHub Pages
experiments.impact: Hannes' Afrikaans voice recordings were not loaded on the live site; Afrikaans fell through to TTS
experiments.fix: Changed to packages/language-data/afrikaans-phrases.js (relative to site root, not repo root)

experiments.name: entities arrays missing in experiences.js
experiments.description: After 7-word acquisition, the entity arrays in experiences.js didn't include the new gap words (pakiaka, ona, kainga, mihi, whakautu)
experiments.impact: Even though entities existed in surface_forms.js, the app couldn't render word chips for them because experiences.js didn't list them
experiments.fix: Added THING_PAKIAKA + PARTICLE_ONA to RIVER_005, THING_KAINGA to RIVER_006, PHRASE_MIHI + PHRASE_WHAKAUTU to RIVER_010
